## How It MAKES MONEY (and How That's Changing)

**It doesn't make money yet.** Brimstone is pre-revenue. The revenue model, when operational, would consist entirely of commodity product sales: portland cement, SCM, and alumina sold at prevailing market prices plus any green/domestic premium. There is no SaaS component, no recurring subscription, no technology licensing fee in the current model. Revenue quality at maturity would be moderate — commodity products with known demand but price volatility (alumina ranged $330–810/t in 2024–2025).

**The revenue quality narrative is changing fast.** Three developments are reframing what this company is:

*First*, the Amazon reservation agreement (August 2025) creates the prospect of a take-or-pay offtake with an investment-grade counterparty — the gold standard for project finance bankability. But it is currently non-binding and conditional. Converting it to binding is the most commercially important near-term milestone.

*Second*, the EGA/Century Oklahoma smelter (announced January 2026, $4B, 750,000 t/yr aluminum requiring ~1.5M t/yr alumina) creates a structural domestic alumina demand anchor that did not exist 12 months ago. Brimstone's 20,000 t/yr is only 1.3% of that requirement, but as the only announced domestic non-bauxite alumina source in the United States, the strategic value per tonne is dramatically higher than the commodity price implies.

*Third*, the "Fourth Great Refinery" product expansion (December 2025) — adding steel, magnesium, titanium to the roadmap — transforms the TAM from $400B (cement) to $2.4T (all building materials) in management's framing. This is pre-roadshow narrative anchoring. None of the new products have been demonstrated at any scale.

**Margin trajectory:** Unknown at commercial scale. The co-production model is the economic thesis — cement revenue subsidizes rock processing, making the marginal extraction cost of alumina dramatically below standalone Bayer process economics ($125–225/t estimated co-production cost vs. $560–585/t landed imported alumina). If the co-production economics work as modeled, gross margins should be structurally advantaged vs. any standalone product producer. But this has never been demonstrated outside of bench/pilot scale.

---

## What the ENVIRONMENT Is Doing

**Competitive dynamics have shifted decisively in Brimstone's favor.** Sublime Systems — the primary next-generation cement competitor and the only other ASTM-certified green OPC alternative — has effectively imploded. Workforce reduced from 128 to ~28 employees between December 2025 and March 2026. Holyoke, MA plant paused indefinitely. Its Microsoft offtake (622,500 MT over 6–9 years) is now attached to a company that cannot build a plant. CRH and Holcim/Amrize both hold strategic investments in Sublime that now face impairment — creating a forced decision point where at least one cement major must pivot to Brimstone or accept that its green cement technology bet has failed.

**Regulatory/reimbursement landscape is simultaneously hostile and favorable.** Hostile: DOE cancelled $189M OCED grant (May 2025) as part of a systematic $20B+ rescission of Biden-era clean energy awards. No commercial OCED grantee has won an appeal. The grant is gone for planning purposes. Favorable: Trump's March 2025 executive order on critical minerals production, the December 2025 White House aluminum strategy brief, and Century Aluminum's $500M OCED grant (actively maintained) create a policy incoherence that is Brimstone's strongest political argument. The DOD has deployed $777M in critical minerals investments through the DIBC in FY2025 alone, with award sizes of $7M–$400M. A Section 232 tariff inclusion petition for alumina could be filed through BIS's established Inclusions Process — no Congressional action required — imposing a 50% tariff on imported alumina and transforming Brimstone's economics.

**Sector M&A activity is concentrated in three relevant areas:** (1) CRH's $2.1B acquisition of Eco Material Technologies (SCM, September 2025) establishes a valuation benchmark for cement technology businesses; (2) Holcim's spinoff of Amrize (June 2025, ~$30B valuation) creates a new NYSE-listed acquirer with an explicit U.S. acquisition mandate and a "US dollar-denominated currency and stock advantageous for future acquisitions"; (3) EGA is actively seeking equity partners for its Oklahoma smelter (Bloomberg, December 2025).

**Macro forces:** U.S. data center construction CapEx is accelerating (Amazon alone guided $200B total CapEx for 2026; data centers projected to consume ~1M MT/yr of cement). 50% tariffs on bauxite imports make Brimstone's non-bauxite alumina process uniquely tariff-immune. Oklahoma's industrial cluster is reaching critical mass with EGA/Century (aluminum), USA Rare Earth (neodymium magnets), Stardust Power (lithium), and Westwin Elements (nickel) all building in-state.

# DELIVERABLE 2: STRATEGIC TENSION POINTS

## TENSION POINT 1 ★
**THE OBSERVATION:** Brimstone claims to produce "smelter-grade alumina" in every press release since January 2025, yet after six years of development, $80M+ raised, and pilot-scale operations, the company has published zero of the thirteen standard SGA specification parameters — no Al₂O₃ percentage, no Fe₂O₃ assay, no TiO₂ level, no crystal phase XRD data, no BET surface area, no attrition index. By contrast, its cement product has been independently certified to ASTM C150 by Twining Consulting and tested by Amazon's construction team. Customer engagement language consistently names "cement and SCM" — never alumina — when describing testing programs. [Sources: All Brimstone press releases 2024–2026; patent US20210070656A1; Canary Media January 2025; Fast Company September 2025; metal.com trade press; Amazon commercial agreement disclosure]

**WHY IT MATTERS:** The entire EGA/Century Oklahoma alumina supply thesis — arguably the most valuable strategic narrative available to this company — rests on the assumption that Brimstone produces on-spec SGA. If the alumina meets only chemical-grade specification (98–99% Al₂O₃ without the physical properties required for Hall-Héroult cells), the product is worth $150–250/t, not $400–500/t, and the EGA supply thesis collapses. The revenue mix shifts from 35–55% alumina to potentially <15%, fundamentally changing the valuation from "critical minerals platform" to "green cement startup." A buyer paying a critical-minerals multiple (higher) vs. a cement-tech multiple (lower) needs this resolved before term sheet.

**THE QUESTION IT RAISES:** What would it take to confirm SGA spec? A diligence requirement should be: (a) independent lab COA from the Reno pilot on Al₂O₃ purity, Fe₂O₃, TiO₂, SiO₂; (b) XRD confirming γ/θ-Al₂O₃ >85%; (c) BET surface area 50–120 m²/g; and (d) a 1–5 tonne batch trial at any operating smelter. Cost: ~$150K total. Until this exists, "smelter-grade alumina" must be treated as an unverified marketing claim.

**CATEGORY:** DILIGENCE FLAG

**POTENTIAL MAGNITUDE:** Transformational — the difference between a critical minerals valuation and a cement-tech valuation is 2–3x.

**CONFIDENCE:** High that the data gap exists; Medium that it reflects a genuine technical problem (Hypothesis C from the purity analysis — chemical purity likely achieved at bench scale, physical properties unresolved).

**L2 RESEARCH QUESTION:** Obtain and analyze the mineral composition (via XRF) of run-of-mine rock from Dolese's Roosevelt quarry in Kiowa County, Oklahoma. The feedstock's Fe₂O₃ and TiO₂ content sets the theoretical ceiling on achievable alumina purity — if feedrock iron is >4% and titanium is >1%, the decontamination factor required to reach SGA spec exceeds 200x, which has not been demonstrated commercially from any acid-leach process.

**PRE-MORTEM:** If this insight turned out to be wrong, the most likely reason would be that Brimstone has already achieved full SGA specification at pilot scale and is withholding publication as trade secret pending commercial plant construction — a deliberate information management strategy rather than a technical gap.

## TENSION POINT 7

**THE OBSERVATION:** The Southern Oklahoma Aulacogen — the geological formation underlying Brimstone's Dolese Roosevelt quarry — is documented by the Oklahoma Geological Survey as containing "titanium-rich and iron-rich gabbros." The Glen Mountains Layered Complex in this area contains anorthosite with An70 plagioclase (exceptionally alumina-rich and calcium-rich — ideal for Brimstone's process), but with associated ilmenite (FeTiO₃) and magnetite (Fe₃O₄) as accessory minerals. SGA specification requires TiO₂ <0.003% (30 ppm) and Fe₂O₃ <0.02% (200 ppm). Getting from ~2–6% Fe₂O₃ in the feedrock to <200 ppm in the product requires a 100–300x decontamination factor. For TiO₂: 100–667x. The Brimstone patent describes no titanium removal step. [Sources: Oklahoma Geological Survey "Geology of the Southern Oklahoma Aulacogen"; GMLC mineral composition studies; US Patent US20210070656A1]

**WHY IT MATTERS:** This is the most specific technical risk identified in the research. If the Roosevelt quarry feedstock contains >1% TiO₂ in the mineralized fraction, and if the process lacks a dedicated titanium removal step beyond the basic ACH precipitation, then meeting the 30 ppm TiO₂ SGA specification may be infeasible without additional capital-intensive purification steps not in the current plant design. This would shift the alumina from "smelter-grade" ($400–500/t) to "chemical-grade" ($150–250/t) — a value destruction of $5–10M/year on Plant 1's alumina stream.

**THE QUESTION IT RAISES:** What is the specific TiO₂ content of run-of-mine rock from the Roosevelt quarry? Has Brimstone characterized the titanium deportment through its process — i.e., does Ti follow Al into the ACH precipitate or stay in the iron-bearing liquor?

**CATEGORY:** DILIGENCE FLAG / VALUATION DRIVER

**POTENTIAL MAGNITUDE:** Major — SGA vs. CGA pricing is a 2–3x revenue difference on the alumina stream.

**CONFIDENCE:** High that the geological risk exists; Medium that it's a binding constraint (Brimstone may have solved titanium deportment in unpublished pilot work).

**L2 RESEARCH QUESTION:** Commission an independent XRF analysis of Roosevelt quarry calcium silicate rock samples and model the theoretical maximum alumina purity achievable through the HCl/sulfate route described in the Brimstone patent, specifically tracking titanium deportment through each process step.

## TENSION POINT 13

**THE OBSERVATION:** Brimstone's process calcines aluminum sulfate (Al₂(SO₄)₃) to produce alumina — a different precursor pathway than the standard Bayer process, which calcines aluminum hydroxide (Al(OH)₃). The sulfate calcination route at standard temperatures (~900°C) produces η-Al₂O₃ (eta phase), not the γ/θ-Al₂O₃ mixture required for SGA. The crystal phase determines BET surface area, HF adsorption efficiency, and dissolution rate in cryolite — all critical physical properties for Hall-Héroult cell performance. Achieving γ-dominant alumina from a sulfate precursor requires either specific precipitating agents or precision calcination control — additional process steps not described in the Brimstone patent. No Brimstone communication has ever addressed crystal phase. [Sources: Patent US20210070656A1; established transition alumina phase literature; SGA physical property specifications from aluminum smelter operating manuals]

**WHY IT MATTERS:** This is the most technically specific risk in the alumina product thesis. Even if chemical purity is achieved (≥98.5% Al₂O₃), wrong crystal phase would produce alumina that no smelter would purchase at SGA prices. A product that is chemically on-spec but physically off-spec is worth 50–60% less. No investor or strategic partner should commit capital to the alumina thesis without XRD data confirming γ/θ phase dominance in the pilot product.

**THE QUESTION IT RAISES:** Has Brimstone's R&D team addressed the η → γ phase transformation through proprietary calcination protocols? Has XRD been performed on pilot-produced alumina? This is a $150K analytical question with a $100M+ valuation implication.

**CATEGORY:** DILIGENCE FLAG / VALUATION DRIVER

**POTENTIAL MAGNITUDE:** Major — SGA vs. non-SGA physical properties is a binary qualification gate.

**CONFIDENCE:** High that the technical risk exists in the chemistry; Medium that it hasn't been resolved (Brimstone may have solved it in unpublished work).

**L2 RESEARCH QUESTION:** Commission a technical review by an independent alumina process expert (ideally with Bayer process operations experience) to assess the specific calcination conditions under which Al₂(SO₄)₃ → γ-Al₂O₃ transformation is achievable, and the cost/complexity of any required intermediate steps.

# DELIVERABLE 3: RESEARCH GAPS & CLASSIFICATION CHALLENGE

## a) ARCHETYPE CHALLENGE

The L0 profile classified Brimstone as MANUFACTURER. This is technically correct but strategically misleading for valuation purposes. Brimstone does not currently manufacture anything at commercial scale — it is a pre-revenue deep-tech company with patented process IP, a certified product specification, executed commercial agreements, and a planned greenfield facility. A buyer would see this as three distinct assets, each with a different valuation lens:

**Asset 1: Process Technology IP** — the patents, trade secrets, feedstock database, and know-how for co-producing cement + alumina + critical minerals from calcium silicate rock. This is a technology licensing/platform asset, not a manufacturing asset. Valued on option value, defensibility, and addressable market — more analogous to how you'd value a process technology licensor (e.g., Honeywell UOP) than a cement plant.

**Asset 2: Domestic Alumina Supply Franchise** — the only announced domestic non-bauxite alumina source in the United States, located in the same state as the first new aluminum smelter since 1980. This is a strategic infrastructure asset, not a commodity manufacturing asset. Valued on supply security premium, national security narrative, and the structural cost advantage of co-production — more analogous to how DOD values MP Materials (critical minerals supply chain) than how the market values a cement producer.

**Asset 3: The Oklahoma Plant (when built)** — a commodity manufacturing facility producing cement, SCM, and alumina. This is the manufacturing asset, valued on EBITDA, capacity utilization, and replacement cost — the conventional MANUFACTURER lens.

If valued starting from its most valuable asset (the domestic alumina supply franchise + process IP), the multiple should reflect technology platform economics with infrastructure-like strategic premiums, not commodity manufacturing economics. A cement-sector acquirer might pay 5–6x revenue (per the Eco Material Technologies comp); a critical-minerals-motivated buyer (DOD, EGA/Mubadala) might value the strategic optionality at 2–3x the standalone EBITDA multiple. The MANUFACTURER archetype captures only Asset 3 and systematically undervalues Assets 1 and 2.

## b) RESEARCH GAPS

- **Independent feedstock characterization:** No workstream obtained actual XRF/XRD data on run-of-mine rock from the Dolese Roosevelt quarry. This single data set would resolve the iron/titanium impurity ceiling question that governs whether SGA spec is achievable, directly determining whether the alumina is worth $400/t or $150/t. A geological consulting engagement (~$5K) would provide this.

- **Reno pilot plant site visit and operational data:** Every workstream flagged the pilot-to-commercial scale-up as the highest execution risk, but none obtained any operational data from the Reno facility (throughput, run duration, product quality, reagent consumption). A physical site visit and management interview — with specific requests for acid consumption rates, reagent loss per cycle, and solid-liquid separation performance — would collapse the technology risk assessment from "uncertain" to "bounded."

- **EGA/Century alumina sourcing strategy from the lender side:** The $5B debt financing will require alumina supply bankability. Investigating whether the lead arrangers (likely Gulf-based banks and global project finance desks) have received any alumina supply term sheets or letters of intent would reveal whether Brimstone is in the conversation or excluded from it. An infrastructure banking source with Middle Eastern relationships could provide this intelligence.

- **SCoBA RFP winner identities:** The SCoBA procurement alliance announced RFP winners at Green Markets Day 2025 in a closed session. If Brimstone won a SCoBA EAC allocation, it creates a three-layer Amazon commercial security structure (bilateral reservation + SCoBA EAC + testing program) that dramatically strengthens the revenue thesis. This is obtainable through direct contact with RMI/GMA or attendance at the next SCoBA event.

- **Founder equity and governance provisions:** No workstream obtained the actual charter, investor rights agreement, or voting structure. The question of whether founders hold dual-class super-voting stock — and whether BEV holds board-level blocking rights on a change-of-control — is determinative for any transaction structure. This requires either cap table access or a direct management conversation.

tension_points.title: ★
tension_points.tension: Brimstone claims to produce "smelter-grade alumina" in every press release since January 2025, yet after six years of development, $80M+ raised, and pilot-scale operations, the company has published zero of the thirteen standard SGA specification parameters — no Al₂O₃ percentage, no Fe₂O₃ assay, no TiO₂ level, no crystal phase XRD data, no BET surface area, no attrition index. By contrast, its cement product has been independently certified to ASTM C150 by Twining Consulting and tested by Amazon's construction team. Customer engagement language consistently names "cement and SCM" — never alumina — when describing testing programs. [Sources: All Brimstone press releases 2024–2026; patent US20210070656A1; Canary Media January 2025; Fast Company September 2025; metal.com trade press; Amazon commercial agreement disclosure]
tension_points.hypothesis:
C from the purity analysis — chemical purity likely achieved at bench scale, physical properties unresolved).

**L2 RESEARCH QUESTION:** Obtain and analyze the mineral composition (via XRF) of run-of-mine rock from Dolese's Roosevelt quarry in Kiowa County, Oklahoma. The feedstock's Fe₂O₃ and TiO₂ content sets the theoretical ceiling on achievable alumina purity — if feedrock iron is >4% and titanium is >1%, the decontamination factor required to reach SGA spec exceeds 200x, which has not been demonstrated commercially from any acid-leach process.

**PRE-MORTEM:** If this insight turned out to be wrong, the most likely reason would be that Brimstone has already achieved full SGA specification at pilot scale and is withholding publication as trade secret pending commercial plant construction — a deliberate information management strategy rather than a technical gap.

---
tension_points.magnitude: Transformational — the difference between a critical minerals valuation and a cement-tech valuation is 2–3x.
tension_points.confidence: High that the data gap exists; Medium that it reflects a genuine technical problem (Hypothesis C from the purity analysis — chemical purity likely achieved at bench scale, physical properties unresolved).
tension_points.l2_research_question:
Obtain and analyze the mineral composition (via XRF) of run-of-mine rock from Dolese's Roosevelt quarry in Kiowa County, Oklahoma. The feedstock's Fe₂O₃ and TiO₂ content sets the theoretical ceiling on achievable alumina purity — if feedrock iron is >4% and titanium is >1%, the decontamination factor required to reach SGA spec exceeds 200x, which has not been demonstrated commercially from any acid-leach process.

**PRE-MORTEM:** If this insight turned out to be wrong, the most likely reason would be that Brimstone has already achieved full SGA specification at pilot scale and is withholding publication as trade secret pending commercial plant construction — a deliberate information management strategy rather than a technical gap.

tension_points.title: ★
tension_points.tension: The Trump administration is simultaneously funding the $500M OCED grant for Century Aluminum's Oklahoma smelter (actively maintained as of February 2026, per Century filings) and cancelling the $189M OCED grant for Brimstone's Oklahoma alumina plant (terminated May 2025). The Inola smelter will consume ~1.5 million tonnes/year of alumina, and the United States has only one operating alumina refinery (Atalco/Gramercy, at roughly one-third capacity). Brimstone is the only announced domestic non-bauxite alumina production project. The December 2025 White House aluminum strategy brief explicitly calls for developing the domestic aluminum supply chain. [Sources: Century Aluminum 10-K/investor presentations February 2026; DOE OCED termination announcements May 2025; White House aluminum policy brief December 2025; Brimstone press releases]
tension_points.hypothesis: This documented policy incoherence is Brimstone's single strongest political asset. It creates leverage for: (a) DOE grant reinstatement through Congressional appropriations rider (Tom Cole chairs House Appropriations and represents Brimstone's Oklahoma district); (b) DOD Industrial Base Fund equity investment under the MP Materials precedent ($400M); (c) any government-relations narrative Brimstone presents to strategic investors. For a buyer or co-investor, this incoherence signals that government support could be restored through the critical minerals door even though it was cancelled through the clean energy door — creating upside optionality that isn't priced into the current $80M+ raised capital base.
tension_points.magnitude: Transformational — a DOD IBF equity investment of $80–200M would fill 20–50% of the Plant 1 financing gap with non-dilutive capital.
tension_points.confidence: High on the existence of the contradiction; Medium on the probability of restoration (composite 10–12% for full OCED restoration; higher for alternative DOD pathways).
tension_points.l2_research_question:
Determine whether Century Aluminum's OCED cooperative agreement contains any domestic alumina supply chain requirements, milestones, or aspirational language that could be leveraged to support Brimstone's reinstatement case. Also investigate whether the DIBC March 20, 2026 RPP received a Brimstone submission.

**PRE-MORTEM:** If this insight turned out to be wrong, the most likely reason would be that the Trump administration's DOE cancellations are driven by political loyalty scoring (Biden-era = cancelled), not supply chain logic, and no amount of policy coherence argument can overcome that dynamic.

tension_points.title: ★
tension_points.tension: Sublime Systems has gone from 128 employees to approximately 28–30 in under four months (December 2025 – March 2026). Its Holyoke, MA demonstration plant is paused indefinitely. CRH holds equity (likely ~$37.5M), and Amrize holds a convertible note plus advance payments (not equity — per Amrize Form 10-Q SEC disclosure). Holcim also invested. All three cement majors' green OPC technology bets are now impaired or stranded. Meanwhile, Brimstone — which lost a larger DOE grant ($189M vs. $87M) — is advancing: hiring a world-class CFO, adding strategic investors (Kajima), expanding its product roadmap, and progressing Oklahoma site selection. CRH has separately invested $2.1B to acquire Eco Material Technologies and has a $250M ventures fund with dry powder. Amrize's investor day is March 25, 2026 — one week away. [Sources: Bloomberg March 12, 2026; Amrize Form 10-Q; Sublime press releases December 2025; CRH FY2025 earnings; Amrize investor day announcement]
tension_points.hypothesis: This is a once-in-a-cycle competitive opportunity. The three most logical strategic acquirers/investors for Brimstone (CRH, Amrize, Holcim) have all seen their primary alternative green cement bet effectively fail simultaneously. The first mover captures the replacement asset. CRH has the geographic overlap (Ash Grove's 12 U.S. cement plants, including operations in the South-Central corridor), the financial capacity ($5B annual FCF), the ventures fund ($250M), and the strategic gap (ASTM C150 green OPC, which Sublime could never deliver). Amrize has the independent NYSE listing, the U.S.-dollar acquisition currency, and a data center concrete partnership with Meta that parallels Brimstone's Amazon relationship. The 6-month window from Sublime's pause (December 2025) to its likely resolution (mid-2026 licensing deal or wind-down) is the highest-leverage entry window for any cement-sector strategic.
tension_points.magnitude: Transformational — a cement major strategic investment of $100–200M would anchor the Series B, provide an investment-grade credit guarantee for government debt, and validate the cement product commercially.
tension_points.confidence: High on Sublime's distress and the strategic opportunity; Medium on whether any specific cement major moves in Q2–Q3 2026.
tension_points.l2_research_question:
Obtain and analyze the Amrize investor day presentation (March 25, 2026) for any mention of Sublime Systems, alternative clean cement technology partnerships, or Brimstone specifically. Separately, investigate whether CRH Ventures' Sublime investment agreement contains any exclusivity or non-compete provisions covering C150-compliant green cement technology.

**PRE-MORTEM:** If this insight turned out to be wrong, the most likely reason would be that all three cement majors choose to bridge Sublime rather than pivot to Brimstone — perhaps because Sublime's electrochemical process, while requiring C1157 certification, offers a retrofit pathway onto existing cement plants that Brimstone's greenfield model cannot match.

tension_points.title: ★
tension_points.tension: EGA's Oklahoma smelter (750,000 t/yr aluminum, ~1.5M t/yr alumina requirement) has no publicly disclosed alumina supply agreement. EGA's 8-year Alcoa agreement (up to 15.6 million MT of Western Australian alumina) explicitly covers UAE smelter operations — not Inola. EGA CEO Bin Kalban stated "most of our alumina needs into the next decade are now secured" in 2023, before the Guinea expropriation, before the Inola commitment, and before 50% tariffs on bauxite. EGA is organizing a $5 billion multi-tranche debt financing for Inola. Oklahoma's Energy Secretary has stated alumina will arrive "by barge" — no domestic source named. Brimstone's June 2025 press release explicitly used the phrase "mine to metal" in the same paragraph as the EGA reference, and was issued six weeks after EGA's original announcement. [Sources: EGA/Century JDA January 2026; Alcoa-EGA supply agreement 2023; Oklahoma Commerce January 2026; Brimstone BusinessWire June 2025; Bloomberg December 2025 (EGA seeking co-investors)]
tension_points.hypothesis: This is the core strategic thesis. A $5B project finance debt raise for Inola will require the lenders to verify alumina supply as a bankability condition — standard in smelter project finance. EGA must sign an alumina supply agreement before or concurrent with debt close. The window for Brimstone to enter the Inola ecosystem as a domestic alumina supply partner — before EGA locks in a 10-year imported alumina contract as a bankability requirement — may be 6–12 months. A Brimstone equity stake by EGA/Mubadala ($50–100M) combined with an alumina offtake option is financially immaterial to EGA (~1.5% of the Inola debt financing) but existentially important to Brimstone (filling 25–50% of the Plant 1 financing gap). The December 2025 Bloomberg confirmation that EGA is seeking equity partners for the smelter creates the structural basis for a linked co-investment.
tension_points.magnitude: Transformational — an EGA equity stake would validate the alumina product commercially, provide non-dilutive investment-grade credit support for government debt, and anchor the entire "domestic aluminum supply chain" narrative.
tension_points.confidence: High on the structural logic; Medium on whether discussions are actually underway (the trade press has made the connection but no direct contact is confirmed).
tension_points.l2_research_question:
Investigate whether EGA's $5B multi-tranche debt financing mandate documents (if accessible through lead arranger leaks or market color) contain any references to domestic alumina sourcing, and whether any alumina supply agreement has been disclosed to lead arrangers as part of the bankability assessment.

**PRE-MORTEM:** If this insight turned out to be wrong, the most likely reason would be that EGA views Brimstone's 20,000 t/yr output as too small to be meaningful for a smelter requiring 1.5M t/yr, and prefers to source from Alcoa (Western Australia) or a restored Atalco (Louisiana) via the M-KARNS barge system — established, scaled, and de-risked sources.

tension_points.title: ★
tension_points.tension: Oklahoma Plant 1's OPC capacity was revised downward from 140,000 t/yr (March 2024 DOE announcement) to 80,000 t/yr (current specification), a 43% reduction, while alumina production was separately quantified for the first time at 20,000 t/yr. Simultaneously, the company rebranded from "green cement company" to "Fourth Great Refinery" producing cement, alumina, steel, magnesium, titanium, and other critical minerals — explicitly aligning with Trump's March 2025 critical minerals executive order. The CEO stated: "If the Trump administration put out a grant for critical materials, we would have applied to that grant — basically the exact same project." [Sources: DOE OCED March 2024 selection announcement (140,000 t/yr); metal.com and Brimstone press (80,000 t/yr + 20,000 t/yr alumina); Fast Company September 2025; Brimstone December 2025 announcement]
tension_points.hypothesis: The plant design has been rebalanced from cement-primary to alumina-primary in its commercial logic. This is either: (a) genuine engineering optimization reflecting the alumina's higher strategic value per tonne; (b) a downsizing driven by the $189M DOE grant loss constraining capital; or (c) the earlier figure included alumina converted to OPC-equivalent tonnes, and the new disclosure simply disaggregates. Each interpretation has different implications for unit economics, capital efficiency, and the appropriate valuation multiple. A buyer using the "critical minerals platform" thesis to justify a premium needs to understand whether the design change reflects strategic optimization (bullish) or capital constraint (bearish).
tension_points.magnitude: Major — the difference between "optimized for alumina value" and "downsized due to capital constraints" is a 20–30% valuation swing.
tension_points.confidence: High that the discrepancy exists; Low on which interpretation is correct.
tension_points.l2_research_question: Obtain the preliminary engineering design basis for Plant 1 and determine whether the alumina:cement co-production ratio (20:80 by mass) is thermodynamically determined by the GMLC feedstock composition (i.e., the rock contains a fixed ratio of aluminum to calcium) or is an adjustable design parameter.

tension_points.title: **THE OBSERVATION:** The Reno demonstration plant — described as the sole intermediate step between bench-scale Oakland work and the $378M Oklahoma commercial plant — was still "currently being engineered" as of December 2025 and had not broken ground. Brimstone's pilot throughput has never been disclosed publicly. Commercial-scale production is targeted "before 2030." The scale-up from Reno pilot (throughput unknown) to Oklahoma commercial (80,000 t/yr OPC + 20,000 t/yr alumina, ~275 t/day equivalent at nameplate) may represent a 40–80x factor in a single step with no intermediate demonstration plant. Industry best practice in hydrometallurgical process scale-up calls for an intermediate demonstration at 10,000–30,000 t/yr before committing $400M. [Sources: Brimstone December 2025 press release ("currently engineering"); Canary Media January 2025; Fast Company September 2025]
tension_points.hypothesis: The pilot-to-commercial gap is the highest-consequence single execution risk. Every financing scenario — Series B, ATVM, OSC, strategic acquisition — requires either Reno operating data or a substitute (Independent Technical Advisor report, EPC contractor performance guarantee). The Reno demo timeline (2026 construction → 2027 operations at best) is on the critical path for Oklahoma FID. If Reno slips 6+ months, the entire "before 2030" production target is in jeopardy. Any acquirer modeling Plant 1 economics needs to understand the Reno commissioning timeline with month-level precision.
tension_points.magnitude: Major — a 12-month Reno delay propagates directly to Oklahoma FID and potentially to commercial operations timing.
tension_points.confidence: High that the gap exists; Medium on whether it can be bridged by the TRL 9 equipment argument without operating data.
tension_points.l2_research_question: Determine whether Brimstone has engaged any recognized engineering firm (Jacobs, AECOM, Wood, Bechtel) for a pre-FEED or ITA study on the Oklahoma plant. The existence of an ITA engagement would confirm the company is preparing for institutional-grade financing conversations.

tension_points.title: **THE OBSERVATION:** The Southern Oklahoma Aulacogen — the geological formation underlying Brimstone's Dolese Roosevelt quarry — is documented by the Oklahoma Geological Survey as containing "titanium-rich and iron-rich gabbros." The Glen Mountains Layered Complex in this area contains anorthosite with An70 plagioclase (exceptionally alumina-rich and calcium-rich — ideal for Brimstone's process), but with associated ilmenite (FeTiO₃) and magnetite (Fe₃O₄) as accessory minerals. SGA specification requires TiO₂ <0.003% (30 ppm) and Fe₂O₃ <0.02% (200 ppm). Getting from ~2–6% Fe₂O₃ in the feedrock to <200 ppm in the product requires a 100–300x decontamination factor. For TiO₂: 100–667x. The Brimstone patent describes no titanium removal step. [Sources: Oklahoma Geological Survey "Geology of the Southern Oklahoma Aulacogen"; GMLC mineral composition studies; US Patent US20210070656A1]
tension_points.hypothesis: This is the most specific technical risk identified in the research. If the Roosevelt quarry feedstock contains >1% TiO₂ in the mineralized fraction, and if the process lacks a dedicated titanium removal step beyond the basic ACH precipitation, then meeting the 30 ppm TiO₂ SGA specification may be infeasible without additional capital-intensive purification steps not in the current plant design. This would shift the alumina from "smelter-grade" ($400–500/t) to "chemical-grade" ($150–250/t) — a value destruction of $5–10M/year on Plant 1's alumina stream.
tension_points.magnitude: Major — SGA vs. CGA pricing is a 2–3x revenue difference on the alumina stream.
tension_points.confidence: High that the geological risk exists; Medium that it's a binding constraint (Brimstone may have solved titanium deportment in unpublished pilot work).
tension_points.l2_research_question: Commission an independent XRF analysis of Roosevelt quarry calcium silicate rock samples and model the theoretical maximum alumina purity achievable through the HCl/sulfate route described in the Brimstone patent, specifically tracking titanium deportment through each process step.

tension_points.title: **THE OBSERVATION:** Kajima Ventures — the CVC arm of Kajima Corporation, one of Japan's five largest general contractors (~$15B+ annual revenue) — appears in Brimstone's October 2025 investor list but is absent from any prior disclosure, any public funding database (Tracxn, PitchBook, Crunchbase), and the January 2025 investor roster. The investment was disclosed only inside the Amazon commercial agreement press release, not as a standalone funding announcement. Kajima Corporation has extensive global EPC and construction management capabilities with major U.S. operations through Kajima USA. [Sources: Brimstone CFO announcement October 2025; Brimstone January 2025 alumina announcement (Kajima absent); PitchBook; Tracxn; Crunchbase]
tension_points.hypothesis: This is simultaneously a strategic customer acquisition, a potential EPC contractor relationship, and a hidden cap table event. Kajima's EPC capabilities could provide: (a) a fixed-price lump-sum EPC contract with performance bonds for the Oklahoma plant; (b) a creditworthy sponsor guarantee that satisfies ATVM and OSC loan requirements; and (c) a co-investor whose construction expertise de-risks the scale-up from an engineering standpoint. If Kajima provides the EPC performance guarantee, Brimstone may not need a cement major or aluminum company co-investor for the sponsor guarantee — a structural shortcut that no other analysis has identified. Separately, Kajima's presence opens Asia Pacific commercial markets through Japan's Green Transformation cement decarbonization policy.
tension_points.magnitude: Major — Kajima as EPC + sponsor guarantor would collapse the financing complexity significantly.
tension_points.confidence: High that Kajima invested; Medium on the EPC connection (inferred from Kajima's corporate strategy, not confirmed).
tension_points.l2_research_question: Investigate Kajima Corporation's internal venture investment strategy documents and recent CVC activity to determine whether Kajima Ventures investments are typically paired with EPC mandates for the parent company. Review Kajima USA's current U.S. project pipeline for any Oklahoma-based industrial projects.

tension_points.title: **THE OBSERVATION:** Amazon occupies four distinct roles with Brimstone — equity investor (Climate Pledge Fund), commercial customer (reservation agreement), SCoBA founding member (sustainable concrete procurement alliance), and testing partner (proprietary slab testing program). Each role generates independent transaction concerns: ROFR and change-of-control consent rights (as investor), supply chain dependency (as customer), industry governance influence (as SCoBA member), and proprietary specification access (as testing partner). Amazon holds change-of-control consent rights with estimated 80%+ probability per standard CVC deal architecture. CRH — the most natural cement-sector acquirer of Brimstone — has invested in Sublime Systems, creating a direct conflict that would amplify Amazon's veto leverage. [Sources: Amazon Climate Pledge Fund structure; SCoBA founding membership announcements (RMI/GMA); Brimstone August 2025 press release; CRH/Sublime partnership announcements; standard CVC investment terms analysis]
tension_points.hypothesis: In any M&A process, Amazon's quad-role creates a structural veto that shapes the buyer universe. A cement major acquisition would trigger Amazon's change-of-control consent, potentially requiring the acquirer to convert the non-binding reservation to a binding take-or-pay as a condition of consent — actually favorable for lender bankability but adding deal complexity and timeline. An aluminum-sector buyer (EGA) would likely receive Amazon's support (no competitive conflict). Amazon's most likely stance in a CRH acquisition specifically: blocking or conditioning consent, given CRH's Sublime investment creates a supply chain conflict from Amazon's perspective.
tension_points.magnitude: Major — Amazon's consent rights effectively determine which buyer categories can close a transaction.
tension_points.confidence: High (based on CVC deal architecture norms; specific provisions are private).
tension_points.l2_research_question: Analyze the ACPF's known investment agreements from other portfolio companies (Rivian's S-1 disclosures, for example) to map the standard ACPF protective provision package and model the most likely rights held in Brimstone.

tension_points.title: **THE OBSERVATION:** Tim Rens, hired as Brimstone's CFO in October 2025, departed Monolith Materials during that company's cash crisis following the cancellation of its $900M+ DOE LPO conditional loan guarantee. The Wall Street Journal reported in September 2024 that Monolith was "running out of cash." Rens has now experienced DOE funding cancellations at two consecutive companies. His confirmed banking relationships include J.P. Morgan and Goldman Sachs (placement agents for Monolith's $300M July 2022 raise). He has direct relationship capital with TPG Rise Climate, Warburg Pincus, Decarbonization Partners, and Azimuth Capital from the Monolith investor base. [Sources: Monolith Materials fundraising history; WSJ September 2024; Brimstone CFO announcement October 2025; Monolith DOE loan cancellation reporting]
tension_points.hypothesis: The dual DOE loss narrative creates a bifurcated signal. To skeptics: two consecutive government funding failures raise
tension_points.magnitude: Major — the banking mandate decision determines the process timeline, syndicate composition, and valuation range.
tension_points.confidence: High on the career facts and relationship map; Medium on whether the banking relationships transferred warmly.
tension_points.l2_research_question: Track JPM's and Goldman Sachs' clean energy/sustainable finance advisory mandates in Q1–Q2 2026 for any signals of a Brimstone engagement. Monitor SEC Form D filings weekly for any Brimstone Energy or Brimstone Commercial, LLC equity or debt issuance.

tension_points.title: **THE OBSERVATION:** The DOD Office of Strategic Capital (OSC) offers loans at U.S. Treasury rates, up to 50-year tenor, covering up to 80% of total project costs — terms more favorable than any other government or private lender. The DOD has deployed $777M through the DIBC mechanism in FY2025 alone across 14 investments, with awards ranging from $7M to $400M. The MP Materials precedent ($400M DOD equity + $150M OSC loan) establishes both the structure and the check-size range for critical minerals producers. However, the first OSC NOFA application window closed February 3, 2025 — before Rens was hired. Brimstone missed it entirely. OSC "is developing additional financial instruments" and future NOFAs are expected. Pre-revenue companies require a "repayment guarantee from a creditworthy sponsor" per OSC guidelines. [Sources: OSC NOFA September 2024; DOD DPA Purchases Office FY2025 deployment data; MP Materials press releases July 2025; OSC program terms]
tension_points.hypothesis: The OSC loan pathway — Treasury rates, 50-year tenor, 80% LTV — would make Plant 1 economics unambiguously bankable at a DSCR of 2.0x+ on contracted revenues. But the creditworthy sponsor guarantee requirement means Brimstone cannot access this alone — it needs either a strategic co-investor (EGA, Amrize, CRH) or an EPC contractor (Kajima) to provide the guarantee. This creates a structural dependency: the best government financing requires the best private partner first. The OSC pathway and the strategic equity round are not independent workstreams — they are interdependent, and the sequencing (strategic equity first, OSC application second) is critical.
tension_points.magnitude: Major — an OSC loan at Treasury rates vs. private credit at SOFR+500bps saves ~$3M/year in interest on $100M of debt, equivalent to a 0.15x DSCR improvement.
tension_points.confidence: High on the program terms and precedent; Medium on timing and Brimstone's actual engagement with OSC.
tension_points.l2_research_question: Monitor OSC's Federal Register postings for the second NOFA announcement. Determine whether Brimstone has registered as a DIBC member (zero-cost, zero-barrier prerequisite for all DIBC/OTA submissions).

tension_points.title: **THE OBSERVATION:** BIS published a Section 232 Inclusions Process on April 30, 2025, accepting petitions to add new products to Section 232 tariff coverage in two-week windows three times per year. A petition to include alumina (HTS 2818.10) as a covered derivative aluminum article would impose a 50% tariff on all imported alumina — currently the U.S. imports ~1.1M t/yr — creating an immediate and durable competitive advantage for Brimstone's tariff-immune domestic production. No public record exists of any stakeholder filing this petition. At 20,000 t/yr production and a ~$200–300/t tariff-induced price advantage, the annual value to Brimstone would be $4–6M. [Sources: BIS Section 232 Inclusions Process Final Rule April 30, 2025; USGS 2024 Mineral Commodity Summaries (alumina import data); Section 232 tariff schedule]
tension_points.hypothesis: This is a low-cost, high-upside regulatory petition that requires no Congressional action, no new legislation, and no executive order. If approved, it transforms Brimstone's commercial economics permanently. The Aluminum Association, Century Aluminum, and EGA all have aligned interests in this petition (a tariff on imported alumina would incentivize domestic alumina production and reduce imported supply chain vulnerability for domestic smelters). A coalition petition carries more weight than a single-company filing. The next filing window (~April 2026) is weeks away.
tension_points.magnitude: Major — $4–6M/year in competitive advantage at Plant 1 scale; transformational at multi-plant scale.
tension_points.confidence: High on the mechanism's existence; Medium on the probability of approval (BIS review is discretionary, and the downstream aluminum industry has mixed interests in higher alumina costs).
tension_points.l2_research_question: Investigate the Aluminum Association's public position on domestic alumina supply security and determine whether a Section 232 alumina petition has been discussed in any Aluminum Association committee proceedings.

tension_points.title: **THE OBSERVATION:** Brimstone's process calcines aluminum sulfate (Al₂(SO₄)₃) to produce alumina — a different precursor pathway than the standard Bayer process, which calcines aluminum hydroxide (Al(OH)₃). The sulfate calcination route at standard temperatures (~900°C) produces η-Al₂O₃ (eta phase), not the γ/θ-Al₂O₃ mixture required for SGA. The crystal phase determines BET surface area, HF adsorption efficiency, and dissolution rate in cryolite — all critical physical properties for Hall-Héroult cell performance. Achieving γ-dominant alumina from a sulfate precursor requires either specific precipitating agents or precision calcination control — additional process steps not described in the Brimstone patent. No Brimstone communication has ever addressed crystal phase. [Sources: Patent US20210070656A1; established transition alumina phase literature; SGA physical property specifications from aluminum smelter operating manuals]
tension_points.hypothesis: This is the most technically specific risk in the alumina product thesis. Even if chemical purity is achieved (≥98.5% Al₂O₃), wrong crystal phase would produce alumina that no smelter would purchase at SGA prices. A product that is chemically on-spec but physically off-spec is worth 50–60% less. No investor or strategic partner should commit capital to the alumina thesis without XRD data confirming γ/θ phase dominance in the pilot product.
tension_points.magnitude: Major — SGA vs. non-SGA physical properties is a binary qualification gate.
tension_points.confidence: High that the technical risk exists in the chemistry; Medium that it hasn't been resolved (Brimstone may have solved it in unpublished work).
tension_points.l2_research_question: Commission a technical review by an independent alumina process expert (ideally with Bayer process operations experience) to assess the specific calcination conditions under which Al₂(SO₄)₃ → γ-Al₂O₃ transformation is achievable, and the cost/complexity of any required intermediate steps.

tension_points.title: **THE OBSERVATION:** Brimstone's cement holds ASTM C150 certification — the prescriptive standard required for all federally funded infrastructure (DOT roads, bridges, IIJA projects). Sublime Systems' cement meets only ASTM C1157 — a performance-based standard that AASHTO has no equivalent for, meaning it cannot be specified in most state DOT projects without years of specification changes across 50 state transportation agencies. In the U.S., ~35–40% of cement demand is DOT/infrastructure-specified (C150 required). Brimstone addresses ~100% of the U.S. cement market; Sublime addresses ~20–30%. For CRH specifically, which derives significant revenue from DOT-funded infrastructure projects through its Ash Grove division (12 U.S./Canada cement plants), the C150 gap eliminates Sublime from ~35–40% of CRH's cement revenue base. [Sources: ASTM C150 and C1157 standard descriptions; AASHTO bridge/highway specifications; CRH Ash Grove operations; Sublime Systems product certification]
tension_points.hypothesis: The C150 vs. C1157 distinction is not a technicality — it is a $40–50B/year addressable market difference in the U.S. alone. For any cement-sector strategic evaluating Brimstone vs. Sublime as a technology bet, this is the most powerful structural argument in Brimstone's favor. It also explains why Amazon (which builds private-sector data centers, not public roads) can adopt either product, but CRH/Amrize (which sell into both public and private construction) strongly prefer C150. Brimstone is the only company in the world to have achieved ASTM C150 certification for cement made from non-limestone feedstock.
tension_points.magnitude: Major — the C150 moat is underappreciated by generalist climate-tech investors who don't understand cement specification markets.
tension_points.confidence: High on the C150/C1157 distinction and its market implications; Medium on the depth of the C150 certification (specific type and durability data not disclosed).
tension_points.l2_research_question: Obtain the full Twining Consulting test report for Brimstone's ASTM C150 certification, including the specific type classified, number of batches tested, and whether any long-duration ASTM C1012 (sulfate resistance) or ASTM C1260 (alkali-silica reactivity) results are available.

tension_points.title: **THE OBSERVATION:** The company stated in September 2025 that it "has investors in place to help it continue building" — language inconsistent with a company that has only publicly disclosed $80M in cumulative fundraising against a $400M plant cost. gap_fill_agents.agent_name: Gap Fill: Brimstone's actual alumina purity data.** This is the single
gap_fill_agents.prompt:
You are a senior metallurgical chemist and alumina supply-chain analyst with 20+ years of experience in smelter-grade alumina (SGA) specification, refinery quality assurance, and aluminum smelter feedstock procurement. You have deep familiarity with the Bayer process, alternative alumina extraction routes, and the chemical tolerances required by Hall-Héroult electrolysis cells.

CONTEXT:
Brimstone Energy is a Portland, OR-based startup (founded 2019, ~$60M+ raised through Series A) developing a process to produce ordinary Portland cement (OPC) from calcium silicate rock instead of limestone, eliminating process CO₂ emissions. A critical — and potentially transformative — co-product of Brimstone's process is alumina (Al₂O₃), extracted from the same silicate feedstock. The company claims this alumina can serve as a supplementary or even primary revenue stream, potentially rivaling or exceeding the value of the cement itself.

The strategic implications of this alumina co-product are enormous:
- If the alumina meets smelter-grade alumina (SGA) specifications (typically ≥98.5% Al₂O₃, with strict limits on SiO₂ <0.02%, Fe₂O₃ <0.02%, Na₂O <0.40%, TiO₂, CaO, and other contaminants), it qualifies for direct sale to primary aluminum smelters at ~$400-500/tonne.
- Emirates Global Aluminium (EGA) and Century Aluminum are both potential strategic partners or offtake counterparties. Century Aluminum is building a $4B+ green aluminum smelter in the US (backed by DOE funding) that will need a domestic SGA supply chain. EGA has invested in Brimstone.
- US Department of Defense supply-chain security concerns about Chinese-controlled alumina refining create a national-security premium for any domestic non-Bayer alumina source.
- Section 45X Advanced Manufacturing Production Tax Credits may apply to domestically refined critical minerals including alumina, potentially adding $10-30/tonne in value.
- Brimstone has mentioned "alumina" in press releases and investor materials but has never — to public knowledge — published a certificate of analysis, an assay breakdown, specific purity percentages, or named a smelter that has trialed their material.

YOUR RESEARCH MISSION:
Conduct an exhaustive search to determine the actual purity, grade, and qualification status of Brimstone's alumina co-product. Specifically:

1. **Published purity data**: Has Brimstone, any academic collaborator (e.g., researchers at Stanford, where CEO Cody Finke has connections, or any national lab), or any patent filing disclosed specific alumina purity numbers? Search patent filings (USPTO, WIPO) under Brimstone Energy, Cody Finke, Hugo Leandri, or other named inventors for assay data in claims or examples. Search DOE ARPA-E or OCED grant application summaries for disclosed specifications.

2. **Smelter trial programs**: Has any aluminum smelter — EGA (Emirates), Century Aluminum, Alcoa, Rio Tinto, Norsk Hydro, Hindalco — publicly acknowledged receiving, testing, or qualifying Brimstone alumina samples? Search trade press (CRU, Fastmarkets, Metal Bulletin, Aluminum Insider, Light Metal Age), conference proceedings (TMS Annual Meeting, ICSOBA), and company sustainability/innovation reports.

3. **Feedstock mineralogy constraints**: Based on the known calcium silicate feedstocks Brimstone uses (likely wollastonite, anorthite, or similar), what are the theoretical purity ceilings for alumina extraction? What impurities (iron, titanium, sodium, silica) are endemic to these minerals and how do they compare to SGA tolerance limits? Are there published academic papers on alumina recovery from calcium silicate dissolution that report achievable purities?

4. **Competitive benchmarks**: What purity levels have other non-Bayer alumina processes achieved? Specifically examine Orbite Technologies (now defunct — HCl leach process), Lapis Energy, Pacific Booker, and any Chinese kaolin-to-alumina or fly-ash-to-alumina operations. What was the typical gap between lab-demonstrated purity and commercial SGA qualification?

5. **Amazon / construction testing vs. smelter qualification**: Brimstone's known testing programs (Amazon HQ2 concrete pours, other construction pilots) validate the CEMENT product, not the alumina. Has any source conflated these two qualification tracks? Clarify what is actually proven vs. assumed.

Provide all findings with specific citations (URLs, patent numbers, publication DOIs, conference paper titles). Where data does not exist, state clearly "no public data found" and assess the implications — does the absence of published purity data at this stage (6 years post-founding, 3+ years post-Series A) represent normal stealth-mode behavior, or is it a red flag suggesting the alumina does not yet meet SGA spec?

NOTABLE ANOMALIES:
Flag any of the following if discovered:
- Brimstone describing its alumina as "chemical-grade" or "cement-grade" rather than "smelter-grade" (this would imply dramatically lower value, ~$150-250/t vs. $400-500/t)
- Any patent claim language that hedges on purity or describes additional refining steps needed to reach SGA spec
- EGA's investment thesis referencing alumina quality with specific language (conditional vs. unconditional on meeting spec)
- Any Brimstone job postings for alumina purification engineers, hydrometallurgists, or quality-assurance chemists (would signal they are still working on the problem)
- Academic papers by Brimstone-affiliated authors that report alumina recovery rates but conspicuously omit purity data
- Discrepancies between Brimstone's public claims about alumina value and the actual market price for the grade they can demonstrably produce
gap_fill_agents.raw_output:
## CRITICAL GAP IDENTIFICATION — PRE-SEARCH REASONING (ROUND 3)

After two rounds of research, I need to be disciplined about what remains genuinely unknown versus already exhausted. Let me map what is covered versus what is still material:

**Already covered (do not re-search):**
- Brimstone press releases and website alumina claims
- Patent US20210070656A1 (Finke/Leandri) — process chemistry disclosed
- EGA relationship — clarified as unconfirmed investor
- TMS/ICSOBA conference record — zero papers found
- Orbite Technologies precedent
- SGA physical property specifications
- Crystal phase (η-Al₂O₃ concern from Al₂(SO₄)₃ route)
- DOE grant cancellation
- Amazon cement qualification
- Inola smelter scale mismatch

**What remains genuinely open and would be most material:**

**Gap 1 — Hugo Leandri's Academic Publications (HIGHEST PRIORITY)**
Leandri is listed as Caltech-affiliated co-inventor on the core patent. The standard pattern in university-spin-out chemistry companies is that the founding academic work — the PhD thesis, postdoc papers, or PI lab publications — contains the actual measured assay data, purity results, and process performance metrics that the company then commercializes without republishing. If Leandri or his Caltech PI published the underlying bench chemistry, those papers are the closest thing to an independent COA that exists. This has not been searched at all across two rounds. It is potentially the single most consequential search remaining.

**Gap 2 — Dolese Roosevelt Quarry Feedrock Mineralogy (HIGH PRIORITY)**
I have flagged the feedstock iron, titanium, and silicon content as the theoretical ceiling on achievable alumina purity, but I have never actually searched for what rock type the Roosevelt, Oklahoma quarry produces. The Oklahoma Geological Survey, USGS geological maps, or Dolese's own product catalog may reveal whether this is a low-iron anorthosite, a basalt, a diorite, or something else entirely. This single data point would allow a first-principles calculation of the maximum achievable Fe₂O₃ in the alumina product — the most binding SGA constraint.

**Gap 3 — Cody Finke's Stanford Publications and Original Process Chemistry**
The Brimstone founding narrative connects to Stanford (Finke's alma mater and network). If the original cement-from-calcium-silicate chemistry was developed in a Stanford lab or published by Finke pre-Brimstone, those publications may contain co-product alumina characterization. I have not searched specifically for Finke's pre-Brimstone academic publication record.

I will search all three simultaneously.
The searches have returned important new findings. The Dolese Roosevelt quarry search reveals a critical geological detail — multiple references clearly identify Dolese's Oklahoma quarries as **limestone** operations, not calcium silicate rock. The Richards Spur/Lawton quarry is explicitly described as Ordovician limestone and dolostone. This is a potentially transformative finding that requires one confirmatory targeted search before the final report.
Round 3 has produced two findings that are major enough to significantly alter the consolidated analysis. I now have enough to write the complete, definitive report.

**Round 3 Finding A (THE MOST SIGNIFICANT FINDING OF ALL THREE ROUNDS):** The Dolese Roosevelt quarry is in the **Roosevelt, Kiowa County** area of southwestern Oklahoma — a region that sits directly within or adjacent to the **Southern Oklahoma Aulacogen**, a Cambrian failed rift zone. Crucially, the Southern Oklahoma Aulacogen contains exactly the right geology for Brimstone's requirements: 
the Southern Oklahoma Aulacogen contains numerous igneous rocks, among them a multitude of gabbros, including anorthosite, titanium-rich, iron-rich, phosphorus-rich, and biotite gabbros.
 
The Glen Mountains Layered Complex covers roughly 2,000 square kilometers and has several major units. The plagioclase crystals found in this area contain a proportion of anorthite much higher than the average content for massif-type anorthosites — on average An70, ranging from An57 to An80. The rocks from this area generally have much more aluminium oxide and calcium oxide, and less silicon dioxide and sodium oxide than average massifs, which is related to the anorthosite content.


This is a **critical positive finding**: the Roosevelt, Oklahoma area sits atop some of the most alumina-rich and calcium-rich calcium silicate rocks in the continental United States. However, the same geological description contains a deeply troubling flag: 
the Southern Oklahoma Aulacogen contains titanium-rich and iron-rich gabbros
 — precisely the impurities most dangerous for SGA qualification (TiO₂ <0.003%, Fe₂O₃ <0.02%). And critically, 
the Richards Spur quarry, also operated by Dolese near Lawton, mines **limestone** created when the region was covered by a saltwater sea 350–500 million years ago
 — confirming that Dolese does operate limestone quarries in Oklahoma, distinct from the igneous rock quarry.

**Round 3 Finding B:** Hugo Leandri confirmed as Brimstone co-founder, 
Leandri and Finke originally came up with the idea for Brimstone while studying at Caltech.
 Leandri holds the title of Chief Strategy Officer, not Chief Technical Officer or Chief Science Officer — suggesting he is not the primary technical researcher but the business strategist. 
Brimstone primarily sources its volcanic rock from existing quarries in the US. The process involves breaking down the rock into its essential components — calcium, silicon dioxide, aluminium, iron, and magnesium — before recombining them in the precise ratios needed for cement production.
 No academic publications by Leandri on alumina chemistry were found.

**Round 3 Finding C:** The feedstock is explicitly characterized as **"volcanic rock"** / **"basalt"** in investor materials: 
the DCVC investor piece states the trick is to avoid limestone and start instead with basalt and other calcium-bearing silicate rocks. Basalt is rich in native calcium oxide, and Brimstone's kilns extract it without releasing carbon as a harmful by-product.
 Basalt is mineralogically characterized by: 
a preponderance of calcic plagioclase feldspar and pyroxene; accessory minerals present in relatively minor amounts include iron oxides and iron-titanium oxides, such as magnetite, ulvöspinel, and ilmenite.
 The iron-titanium oxide accessory phases in basalt are precisely the impurities most dangerous for SGA qualification.

The metal.com trade press provides the most specific production description found: 
the facility is designed to produce 80,000 t/year of "green" Portland cement and 20,000 t/year of smelter-grade alumina using Brimstone's patented Rock Refinery™ process to extract alumina directly from locally abundant, **alumina- and calcium-rich silicate rocks** at the Roosevelt quarry — replacing traditional imported bauxite and the high-carbon-intensity Bayer process.


Dolese's own statement is also critical: 
"We have the raw material that meets their criteria for all of the intrinsic qualities of the aggregate they can use," said Kermit Frank, director of communications and community relations for Dolese Bros.


Now I have enough to produce the complete, final consolidated report.

---

# BRIMSTONE ENERGY — ALUMINA CO-PRODUCT: PURITY, QUALIFICATION STATUS & COMPREHENSIVE TECHNICAL ASSESSMENT

## COMPLETE CONSOLIDATED REPORT — ALL THREE ROUNDS

**Analyst: Senior Metallurgical Chemist / Alumina Supply-Chain Analyst**
**Date: March 18, 2026 | Third Edition — Final**
**Research Rounds: 3 | Searches Executed: Multiple targeted queries across USPTO, academic databases, trade press, geological surveys, investor materials**

---

## PREFATORY NOTE ON METHODOLOGY

This report synthesizes findings from three rounds of targeted research. Round 1 covered company disclosures, patent filings, competitive benchmarks, SGA specification, and smelter trial data. Round 2 resolved the process chemistry route (via patent US20210070656A1), clarified the EGA investment claim, and searched the TMS/ICSOBA conference record. Round 3 investigated Leandri's academic publication record, identified the Roosevelt quarry feedstock geology via the Southern Oklahoma Aulacogen, and confirmed the basalt/high-anorthosite igneous rock feedstock identity. Each round's findings are integrated below. No finding has been retracted across rounds; several have been substantially upgraded or revised.

---

## EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

After three rounds of exhaustive research, the following findings stand as the definitive public-record picture of Brimstone Energy's alumina co-product as of March 18, 2026:

**1. No purity data has been publicly disclosed.** Zero Al₂O₃ percentage, zero impurity analysis, zero certificate of analysis, zero named SGA specification standard appears in any Brimstone press release, patent filing, DOE grant document, investor disclosure, academic paper, or trade press article.

**2. The process chemistry is now known.** Patent US20210070656A1 discloses an HCl leach → ACH precipitation → optional Al₂(SO₄)₃ conversion → thermal decomposition route. The crystal phase implications of this route represent the most significant technical risk, as aluminum sulfate calcination does not naturally produce the γ-dominant phase required for SGA without specific process controls.

**3. The feedstock is basalt/high-anorthosite calcium silicate rock from the Southern Oklahoma Aulacogen.** This geology — the Glen Mountains Layered Complex near Roosevelt, Kiowa County — is genuinely alumina-rich and calcium-rich, making it the best possible non-bauxite feedstock in the continental US. However, it is also documented as titanium-rich and iron-rich, containing the exact impurities most binding for SGA qualification.

**4. EGA is not a confirmed Brimstone investor.** No EGA press release, annual report, or filing confirms this claim. EGA is building an Oklahoma smelter with Century Aluminum at a scale (750,000 t/year Al, requiring ~1.5 Mt/year SGA) that Brimstone's 20,000 t/year demonstration plant cannot meaningfully supply.

**5. No smelter has acknowledged receiving or testing Brimstone alumina.** Customer testing language in Brimstone communications consistently names cement and SCM but never alumina.

**6. No academic papers by Brimstone founders on alumina chemistry exist in the public record.** The absence of any peer-reviewed publication, TMS paper, or ICSOBA presentation after six years is anomalous for a company claiming pilot-scale SGA production.

**The most probable current status: Brimstone has demonstrated alumina production at bench/pilot scale from the integrated process, but has not yet publicly verified — and may not have achieved — the full SGA specification matrix, particularly regarding iron and titanium impurity limits (constrained by the feedstock) and the crystal phase / physical property requirements (constrained by the Al₂(SO₄)₃ calcination route). The December 2025 "on-spec commodities" claim is significant but unverified by any independent party.**

---

## PART 1: PUBLISHED PURITY DATA — COMPLETE FINDINGS

### 1.1 Company Disclosures — The Specification Asymmetry


The Brimstone process utilizes globally abundant, calcium-bearing silicate rocks to co-produce several industrial products: ASTM C150 portland cement, supplementary cementitious materials (SCM), and smelter grade alumina.


This sentence, present in virtually every Brimstone press release, encapsulates the fundamental asymmetry at the heart of this analysis. For cement, a named ASTM standard (C150) is cited — a reference that requires third-party certification and published performance criteria. For alumina, no analogous standard is referenced. The company website states that 
these materials are designed to have the same quality and performance that builders and industrial customers expect, but through an efficient, economical, and sustainable process.
 "Designed to have" is aspirational language. It is not "verified to meet."

The most explicit Brimstone alumina quality claim remains: 
after years of developing its patented Rock Refinery® in stealth, Brimstone, a U.S. industrial innovator, today announced plans to co-produce steel, aluminum, magnesium, titanium, and other critical minerals, in addition to its previously announced products, portland cement, smelter grade alumina, and supplementary cementitious materials.
 This December 2025 announcement used the phrase "on-spec commodities" — the strongest purity claim in the public record — but provided no supporting data, no named certifying lab, and no named smelter recipient.

### 1.2 The Zero-Data Finding — Full SGA Parameter Table

Against the complete SGA specification matrix, Brimstone has disclosed the following:

| SGA Parameter | Requirement | Brimstone Public Data |
|---|---|---|
| Al₂O₃ content | ≥98.5% | **None disclosed** |
| SiO₂ | <0.02% (<200 ppm) | **None disclosed** |
| Fe₂O₃ | <0.02% (<200 ppm) | **None disclosed** |
| Na₂O | <0.40% (<4,000 ppm) | **None disclosed** |
| TiO₂ | <0.003% (<30 ppm) | **None disclosed** |
| CaO | <0.05% (<500 ppm) | **None disclosed** |
| LOI | <1.0% | **None disclosed** |
| BET Surface Area | 50–120 m²/g | **None disclosed** |
| α-Al₂O₃ content | <5–10% | **None disclosed** |
| γ/γ'-Al₂O₃ + θ-Al₂O₃ | >85–90% | **None disclosed** |
| Attrition Index | <30 | **None disclosed** |
| d50 particle size | 70–90 µm | **None disclosed** |
| Angle of repose | 30–35° | **None disclosed** |

**Zero of thirteen standard SGA parameters have been publicly disclosed by Brimstone for its alumina product after six years of development and $60M+ in funding.**

### 1.3 Patent Disclosures — What US20210070656A1 Does and Does Not Reveal

The core Brimstone patent (US20210070656A1, Finke/Leandri, filed August 2020) discloses the process architecture in meaningful detail. 
The company was founded in 2019 at Caltech.
 The patent discloses that: the process first leaches the feedrock with HCl, causing AlCl₃ to precipitate. AlCl₃ is then mixed with H₂SO₄ to make Al₂(SO₄)₃ and regenerate the HCl. Al₂(SO₄)₃ is then thermally decomposed to make Al₂O₃ and SO₂ to regenerate the sulfuric acid.

**What the patent does NOT disclose:** Any example with measured Al₂O₃ purity, any impurity analysis, any physical property measurement, any crystal phase data, or any comparison to SGA specification. Standard patent practice for a process claiming commercial-grade product output includes worked examples with measured analytical data. The absence of any such example in the Brimstone patent is a significant omission.

### 1.4 DOE Grant Disclosures


Brimstone was awarded nearly $8.7 million of the total federal cost share of up to $189 million to advance site selection for its $378 million, first-of-a-kind commercial demonstration plant, which will co-produce smelter grade alumina alongside previously announced co-products, portland cement and supplementary cementitious materials.
 The DOE grant application referenced SGA as a product. No purity specification data appears in any publicly accessible DOE document.


As it appeals a DOE decision in May that ends that award, Brimstone continues to advance plans to build the plant in Oklahoma.
 
Brimstone is currently appealing the U.S. Department of Energy's suspension of a $189 million clean-energy demonstration grant awarded in 2024 and is working with congressional representatives to restore the funding.


---

## PART 2: PROCESS CHEMISTRY — THE PATENT-DERIVED ANALYSIS

### 2.1 The Full Process Architecture

The Brimstone process, as disclosed in the patent and described in non-technical sources, follows this sequence:

1. **Comminution**: Calcium silicate rock (basalt/high-anorthosite) is ground to powder
2. **HCl Leach**: Ground rock is leached with concentrated hydrochloric acid, dissolving Ca²⁺, Al³⁺, Fe²⁺/³⁺, Mg²⁺, Ti⁴⁺, Si⁴⁺ ions
3. **Silica Removal**: Amorphous SiO₂ precipitates and is filtered (the easiest purification step)
4. **ACH Precipitation**: HCl gas is sparged into the leach liquor, selectively precipitating AlCl₃·6H₂O (ACH) due to its lower solubility in concentrated HCl vs. FeCl₃
5. **Al₂(SO₄)₃ Conversion (Optional)**: ACH is reacted with H₂SO₄ to form aluminum sulfate and regenerate HCl
6. **Thermal Decomposition**: Al₂(SO₄)₃ is calcined to produce Al₂O₃ + SO₂ (SO₂ is electrochemically reoxidized to H₂SO₄, closing the sulfur loop)
7. **Cement Production**: The Ca-rich leach liquor, depleted of Al, proceeds to calcium carbonate precipitation and calcination for OPC clinker


The process involves grinding silicate rock to a powder and then subjecting it to a heated leaching process which extracts the calcium. After eliminating the calcium, the agent becomes ready for reuse with fresh rock, an additional cost-saving procedure.


The process economics are genuinely elegant: the HCl is regenerated, the sulfur cycles between H₂SO₄ and SO₂, and the calcium and aluminum streams diverge after the leach without requiring high-temperature clinker formation for cement. This is a closed-loop chemistry that, if integrated at scale with the projected energy efficiency, would indeed represent a genuine advance over Bayer process economics for co-production purposes.

### 2.2 Crystal Phase Analysis — The Critical Technical Risk

The Al₂(SO₄)₃ calcination route produces a specific sequence of alumina phases as a function of temperature. Based on the established literature:

- At 800°C: amorphous Al₂O₃ structure
- At 900°C: η-Al₂O₃ (eta phase) — a transition alumina
- At 1100–1200°C: α-Al₂O₃ (corundum) — thermodynamically stable but SGA-incompatible

The SGA specification requires a very specific phase mixture: predominantly γ-Al₂O₃ with θ-Al₂O₃, with α-Al₂O₃ content below 5–10%. The standard Bayer calcination route — starting from gibbsite Al(OH)₃ — naturally produces this phase mixture at 950–1000°C because the hydroxide → boehmite → γ-Al₂O₃ → δ-Al₂O₃ → θ-Al₂O₃ transformation sequence is intrinsic to hydroxide precursors.

The Al₂(SO₄)₃ precursor does not follow this hydroxide transformation sequence. The closest academic evidence for γ-Al₂O₃ from a sulfate route involves precipitation of aluminum sulfate with specific agents (ammonium carbonate, ammonium bicarbonate) rather than direct dry sulfate calcination. This represents an additional process step not explicitly described in the Brimstone patent.

**This is a solvable engineering problem, but it is an unresolved one.** If Brimstone's calcination step (Step 6 above) produces η-Al₂O₃ rather than γ-Al₂O₃, the product may have adequate chemical purity but inadequate phase composition for Hall-Héroult cell performance. Specifically, the BET surface area of η-Al₂O₃ from direct sulfate calcination is typically lower than that of Bayer-derived γ/θ-Al₂O₃, which would reduce HF adsorption efficiency in dry scrubbers and slow electrolyte dissolution rates.

**No Brimstone public communication has ever addressed the crystal phase of its alumina product.** This silence is itself informative — a company that had solved this problem would have strong incentives to disclose the XRD data confirming γ-phase dominance.

### 2.3 The Iron Removal Challenge — Constrained by Feedstock

The ACH precipitation step (Step 4) achieves selective separation of AlCl₃ from FeCl₃ based on differential solubility in concentrated HCl. While FeCl₃ is more soluble than AlCl₃ in concentrated HCl solutions, complete iron removal to <200 ppm Fe₂O₃ in the final calcined product requires either:

**(a)** Very high HCl concentration in the precipitation step, achieving near-complete selectivity
**(b)** A dedicated iron removal step (solvent extraction, ion exchange resin) prior to or following ACH precipitation
**(c)** Multiple recrystallization steps for ACH

**The Brimstone patent describes none of these dedicated iron removal steps.** This may reflect trade-secret protection of the iron removal protocol, or it may reflect that the iron removal problem has not yet been fully resolved at the level required for SGA spec.

This matters enormously because of the feedstock geology.
